subject: Mathematics
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: ALGEBRA
topics: In Form 1, learners were introduced to the basics of algebra, such as variables, constants, coefficients, and
terms, and they practiced simplifying expressions. To build on this foundation, in form 2, learners will now focus
on the basic processes of algebra.
Key terms: Monomial, Binomial, Linear expression, Quadratic expression, Simplification, Factorization
activities: (monomial by binomial and binomial by binomial)
Scenario: Chanda goes to a shop to buy school exercise books and pens. Each exercise book costs k8, but the
learner does not know how many exercise books she will buy. Each pen costs k3, and she buys 4 pens.
a. Let x represent the number of exercise books bought, write an expression for the total cost of the exercise
b. Given that the total cost of the books and pens can be written as 4(2𝑥 + 3), compare the expression in
(a) with this one. What steps would we take to convert the expression in (b) to the expression in (a)
